framework.name: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications
framework.description: Application-layer risks in LLMs and generative AI: prompt injection, insecure output handling, supply chain, and agentic risks.

controls.title: Prompt Injection
controls.description: Manipulating LLM behavior via crafted inputs to bypass guardrails or exfiltrate data. Implement sanitization, parameterization, and privilege separation.
controls.implementationGuidance: Sanitize and parameterize prompts; implement guardrails; validate outputs before execution.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Document input validation rules, guardrail configurations, and test results.

controls.title: Insecure Output Handling
controls.description: Unvalidated LLM output leading to code injection, XSS, or downstream exploits. Treat all LLM output as untrusted.
controls.implementationGuidance: Validate and sandbox all outputs; no direct execution; output encoding.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Output validation logic, sandbox configuration, security tests.

controls.title: Training Data Poisoning
controls.description: Malicious or biased data in training sets affecting model behavior. Requires data provenance and vetting.
controls.implementationGuidance: Data lineage tracking; vet datasets and trusted sources; bias testing.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Data provenance docs, vetting records, bias assessment results.

controls.title: Model Denial of Service
controls.description: Resource exhaustion via costly prompts or excessive requests. Implement rate limiting and monitoring.
controls.implementationGuidance: Rate limiting, quota enforcement, input length limits, anomaly detection.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Rate limit configs, monitoring dashboards, incident logs.

controls.title: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Compromised models, plugins, or dependencies from untrusted sources. SBOM and dependency scanning required.
controls.implementationGuidance: SBOM; dependency scanning; vet models and datasets; cryptographic signing.

controls.title: Sensitive Information Disclosure
controls.description: LLM revealing confidential data from training or context. Implement least privilege and context constraints.
controls.implementationGuidance: Least privilege; token/memory constraints; data classification; PII redaction.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Access controls, redaction configs, classification schema.

controls.title: Insecure Plugin Design
controls.description: Plugins with excessive permissions or weak authorization. Enforce least-privilege and explicit approval.
controls.implementationGuidance: Least-privilege plugins; explicit user approval for sensitive actions; secure orchestration.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Plugin permission matrix, approval workflows, security reviews.

controls.title: Excessive Agency
controls.description: Agents taking unintended high-impact actions without adequate oversight. Human-in-the-loop and bounded execution.
controls.implementationGuidance: Human-in-the-loop for high-risk actions; bounded memory; agent RBAC; no root access.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Agent policies, approval workflows, audit logs.

controls.title: Overreliance
controls.description: Blind trust in LLM output without validation or fallbacks. Implement verification and human review.
controls.implementationGuidance: Validate critical outputs; fallback procedures; human review for high-stakes decisions.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Validation logic, fallback playbooks, review workflows.

controls.title: Model Theft
controls.description: Unauthorized extraction of model weights or parameters. Rate limiting, watermarking, access controls.
controls.implementationGuidance: Rate limiting on endpoints; watermarking; model signing; access controls and monitoring.
controls.evidenceGuidance: API rate limits, watermarking implementation, access logs.
